sources: docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md V2 (cited) and V5 attack flag
docs/core/DECISIONS.md #17 (ratified): 'authenticated by signatures from the current owner key'
docs/core/DECISIONS.md #50 recovery-auth (Status: PROVISIONAL — pending DK ratification, 2026-06-13)

inputs.attackFlagText: 'Current owner key' is undefined mid-recovery (during an open challengeWindowBlocks window) until recovery-auth is ruled — an attacker could race records in that window; the R* area owns the state definition, this predicate consumes it.

fixture.caseRecordByPreRecoveryOwnerDuringWindow: a value record signed by the PRE-recovery owner key, submitted while a recovery challenge window is OPEN; under recovery-auth #50-b1 (interval opens at invocation) the pre-recovery owner is no longer current -> REJECT (closes the mid-recovery record-race)
fixture.recoveryState: RecoverOwner invoked, challengeWindowBlocks open; #50-b1 current draft = interval (and thus the current owner key) opens at invocation
fixture.priorState: a name with an in-flight recovery; pre-recovery owner key still holds the stolen credential

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on recovery-auth (#50): the V2 predicate (reject unless ownerPubkey == the interval's CURRENT owner key) is ratified via #17, but WHICH key is current during an open recovery challenge window is determined by #50 — whether RecoverOwner opens a new interval at invocation (#50-b1, current draft) or only at window-close. #50 is PROVISIONAL pending DK. The R* area owns the recovery state shape; this predicate consumes it, so the verdict flips with the #50 ruling. Authored under #50-b1 (interval opens at invocation -> pre-recovery owner not current -> reject); flips per flipMarker. NOT vector-now.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; flips on DK's #50 ruling.

expected.invariant: a value record is authentic only if signed by the CURRENT owner key for the interval; under recovery-auth #50-b1 (interval opens at invocation) a record signed by the pre-recovery owner key during an open recovery challenge window is no longer current and is rejected — closing the mid-recovery record-race; identical on deterministic replay (Decision #17 fixes 'current owner key authenticates'; #50 fixes WHICH key is current mid-recovery)
expected.rationale: The V2 predicate (reject unless ownerPubkey == current interval's owner key) is ratified (Decision #17), but WHICH key is 'current' during an open recovery challenge window depends on recovery-auth (#50): whether RecoverOwner opens a new interval at invocation or only at window-close determines whose key is current. #50 is PROVISIONAL pending DK. The R* area owns the state shape; this predicate consumes it, so the verdict flips with the #50 ruling.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK rules recovery-auth toward window-close opening (the interval opens only at challenge-window close, not at invocation), the pre-recovery owner key remains current DURING the window and this same record ACCEPTS (verdict flips reject->accept); the #17 'current-owner-key authenticates' shape is stable, only which key is current mid-recovery moves with the #50 ruling.

inputs.attackFlagText: Whether RecoverOwner opens a new interval at invocation or only at challenge-window close determines which ref is 'current' mid-recovery — cannot be pinned until recovery-auth is ruled.

fixture.caseStalePreRecoveryRef: a value record whose ownershipRef equals the PRE-recovery interval ref, submitted during an open challenge window; under recovery-auth #50-b1 (interval opens at invocation) the current ref moved to the recovery-invocation txid, so the pre-recovery ref is stale -> REJECT
fixture.caseRefAtInvocation: the paired case: a record whose ownershipRef equals the recovery-invocation txid ACCEPTS under #50-b1 (this is the neg/pos pair; the verdict swaps under window-close opening)
fixture.recoveryState: RecoverOwner invoked, challengeWindowBlocks open; #50-b1 current draft = current interval ref is the recovery-invocation txid
fixture.priorState: a name with an in-flight recovery; two candidate ownershipRefs (pre-recovery interval vs recovery-invocation txid)

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on recovery-auth (#50): V5 is candidate-stays and explicitly 'Blocked on decision: recovery-auth.' Which interval reference is 'current' mid-recovery is fixed only once #50 rules whether RecoverOwner opens the interval at invocation (#50-b1, current draft) or at window-close. The candidate-tier authority for the interval-reference shape (WIRE §8.1 ownershipRef) exists, so the verdict is derivable once #50 is fixed; until then provisional. Authored under #50-b1 (current ref = recovery-invocation txid -> pre-recovery ref stale -> reject); the neg/pos pair swaps per flipMarker. NOT vector-now.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps + flipMarker; status proposed pending reviewer sign-off; the neg/pos pair swaps on DK's #50 ruling.

expected.invariant: a value record's ownershipRef must equal the CURRENT interval reference; under recovery-auth #50-b1 (interval opens at invocation) the current ref mid-recovery is the recovery-invocation txid, so a record carrying the pre-recovery interval ref is stale and is rejected; identical on deterministic replay (#50 fixes which ref is current mid-recovery; WIRE §8.1 fixes the ownershipRef encoding)
expected.rationale: V5 is candidate-stays and explicitly 'Blocked on decision: recovery-auth.' Which interval reference is 'current' mid-recovery is determined only once recovery-auth (#50, PROVISIONAL) rules whether RecoverOwner opens the interval at invocation or at window-close. The candidate-tier authority for the interval-reference shape exists, so the verdict is derivable once the #50 ruling is fixed; until then it is provisional.

flipMarker.flipsTo: rederive: if DK rules recovery-auth toward window-close opening, the pre-recovery interval ref stays current THROUGH the window — the stale-ref record then ACCEPTS and a record keyed to the recovery-invocation txid is the one rejected (the neg/pos pair swaps); the ownershipRef-must-equal-current-ref shape (WIRE §8.1) is stable, only which ref is current mid-recovery moves with the #50 ruling.
